textDisplay: People say they shouldn't trust trailers because they give the false impression that a movie is good when it really sucks. But the opposite could be true as well. Some trailers can make movies look absolutely awful until you watch them and discover they're not... you discover that they're better than expected. The bottom line is, this movie could suck, but you'll never know until the reviews and box office numbers come in. It's interesting  that some of the commentators here say they thought it was a comedy at first or that it should be a comedy, because horror and comedy are harder to do than other genres. You always have to hit the right note with them. Likewise, the viewing audience is so hard to please with horror and comedy. Not everyone finds the same thing scary. Not everyone finds the same thing funny. We'll see if this hits the right note.

Trickster spirits can come through, and like any spirit it can feed off your energy, fear is the easiest way to get your energy. It will do anything it can to continue to feed, including attaching itself to you or your home, and moving objects when it gains enough power. And who is typically called at this point? Either a priest, or witches like me. Can you blame me for being tired of this when we have to clean up everyones mess because movies like this exist? There wasn't mass reports of Ouija-based possesions and hauntings until after the first movie came about, because people didn't believe it could hurt them, it was just a game. The first "possession" before the movie was even made was confessed to be a hoax by many of the people who tried to "cure" the victim.
